description: Benchmark global multi-region synchronization via regional bridge.

comparison_profile.objective: Compare regional isolation and bridge replication against the single-region presence design.

workload_model.payload_shape: Same fixed-size benchmark payload as earlier labs.
workload_model.concurrency_model: Deterministic virtual users with one message every 1000 ms in the comparison run.

metrics_contract.latency: Client-observed end-to-end latency for the selected regional endpoint.
metrics_contract.error_rate: Dropped messages plus DB errors divided by total processed messages.
metrics_contract.db_latency: Optional, reported only when a persistence path exists.

traceability.contract: trace_id and message_id survive regional ingest, bridge forwarding, and remote rebroadcast.
traceability.propagation: Client -> local region -> bridge -> remote region -> client echo.

consistency_model.notes: Local latency remains fast, while remote visibility is asynchronous and physically bounded.

failure_model.focus: Bridge lag, replication backlog, and regional isolation during partial failure.

routing_strategy.policy: Latency-based regional affinity with asynchronous bridge replication.
routing_strategy.objective: Keep local traffic local and push global consistency off the synchronous path.

observability.baseline: Prometheus metrics on regional nodes and the bridge service.
observability.logging: Event IDs provide the cross-component contract for following a message between regions.

cost_model.dominant_axis: Cross-region egress and duplicated regional infrastructure.
cost_model.notes: Geography introduces network and operational cost that scale with replication volume.

synthesis.scaling_inflection: Geography forces a formal consistency trade-off and a formal routing policy.
synthesis.learning_goal: Make regional routing and eventual global convergence explicit.

comparison_standard.description: Fair cross-lab comparison scenario with fixed duration and payload size.

global_scaling.description: Benchmark global multi-region synchronization via regional bridge.
